source_name: Code4rena Dyad Findings and Analysis Report (2024-04)
finding_title: VaultManagerV2.sol::burnDyad function is missing an isDNftOwner modifier, allowing a user to burn another user’s minted DYAD
summary: VaultManagerV2.sol has a function burnDyad that allows a DNft owner to burn his minted DYAD tokens.
root_cause: missing validation or protection in VaultManagerV2.sol::burnDyad function is missing an isDNftOwner modifier, allowing a user to burn another user’s minted DYAD, leaving the affected path without the required safety check
broken_invariants: liquidation and collateral-ratio checks must not be bypassed or distorted by the root cause: missing validation or protection in VaultManagerV2.sol::burnDyad function is missing an isDNftOwner modifier, allowing a user to burn another user’s minted DYAD, leaving the affected path without the required safety check
users must retain a reliable exit path unless an explicitly designed and validated pause or restriction is active
privileged, owner, and license checks must be enforced on every value-moving path, not only on the nominal entry point
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

impact.description: VaultManagerV2.sol has a function burnDyad that allows a DNft owner to burn his minted DYAD tokens.

why_not_higher: final C4 severity is Medium; exploitability, affected value, or preconditions are bounded relative to High
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: Add isDNftOwner modifier to VaultManagerV2.sol::burnDyad to check if the passed DNft id belongs to msg.sender, preventing the function caller from being able to burn another user’s minted DYAD. function burnDyad(uint256 id, uint256 amount) external isValidDNft(id) + isDNftOwner(id) { dyad.burn(id, msg.sender, amount); emit BurnDyad(id, amount, msg.sender); }
